more.title: How the Skeleton of Mutapa Times is Based on Sarah Charlesworth's Work
more.description: Conceptual artist Sarah Charlesworth's 1979 study of how newspapers covered a solar eclipse inspired the framework behind The Mutapa Times.

more.title: The Mirage of Muzarabani: The Distance Between Discovery and Delivery
more.description: The Muzarabani gas discovery is being presented to Zimbabweans as a moment of arrival, a geological vindication that the country sits atop untapped energy wealth capable of transforming its economy.

more.title: Zanu PF MP faces money laundering rap after CDF cash flows to his companies
more.description: Mberengwa East lawmaker allegedly funnels funds to microfinance firm which paid out US dollars Source: Zanu PF MP faces money laundering rap after CDF cash flows to his companies – Zimbabwe News Now Mberengwa East MP Tasara Hungwe HARARE – Tasara Hungwe, the Zanu PF MP for Mberengwa East, is under investigation for allegedly diverting [...] The post Zanu PF MP faces money laundering rap after CDF cash flows to his companies appeared first on Zimbabwe Situation .

more.title: Harare CBD Bus Terminals Overrun By Vendors, Street Kids, Homeless Adults
more.description: Harare’s Central Business District (CBD) bus terminals are increasingly descending into chaos, with vendors, street children, and homeless adults turning them into makeshift settlements, raising serious safety and sanitation concerns for commuters.

more.title: Biti released on $500 bail
more.description: Source: Biti released on $500 bail JUST IN | Magistrate Musiiwa has granted Tendai Biti and Morgan Ncube bail of US$500 each, under strict conditions. The defense argued against some of the conditions, but the state insisted that they remain in place. The bail conditions are as follows: – They must surrender their passports – [...] The post Biti released on $500 bail appeared first on Zimbabwe Situation .

more.title: 5 000t rice boosts food security
more.description: Source: 5 000t rice boosts food security – herald Precious Manomano and Muchaneta Chimuka Zimbabwe has received 5 000 tonnes of rice from China as part of efforts to address food insecurity among vulnerable populations. The consignment, handed over at the Grain Marketing Board in Harare, will augment Government efforts to mitigate effects of recent [...] The post 5 000t rice boosts food security appeared first on Zimbabwe Situation .

more.title: Uhuru preps a gear up
more.description: Source: Uhuru preps a gear up – herald Thupeyo Muleya Beitbridge Bureau MATABELELAND South Provincial Affairs and Devolution Minister Albert Nguluvhe yesterday toured legacy projects in Beitbridge as part of the province’s preparations to host Independence Day celebrations in Matobo District. One of the key sites he visited was the Zezani Assembly Point, also known [...] The post Uhuru preps a gear up appeared first on Zimbabwe Situation .

more.title: Mutapa assets valued at ZiG16bn
more.description: Source: Mutapa assets valued at ZiG16bn – herald Farirai Machivenyika Senior Reporter The Mutapa Investment Fund’s gross assets were valued at ZiG16 billion as at December 31 last year, following a valuation exercise carried out by several independent auditors. This was revealed by MIF chief executive Dr John Mangudya yesterday when he appeared before Parliament’s [...] The post Mutapa assets valued at ZiG16bn appeared first on Zimbabwe Situation .

more.title: Who will drive the bus: Mamvura, General Chiwenga, or someone else? Will President Mnangagwa retire peacefully? (Part 2)
more.description: In this second and final part of the article, I continue to examine the potential outcomes of ZANU-PF’s succession politics, focusing on whether Kudakwashe Tagwirei (whom I metaphorically refer to as “Mamvura”) will succeed in his presumed bid for the presidency, whether General Constantino Chiwenga will recover his political standing and take over, whether someone else will ascend to the throne, and whether President Mnangagwa will ultimately retire in peace. The post Who will drive the bus: Mamvura, General Chiwenga, or someone else? Will President Mnangagwa retire peacefully? (Part 2) appeared first on Nehanda Radio .

more.title: Biti’s arrest a cowardly act says Ugandan opposition leader Bobi Wine
more.description: EXILED Ugandan opposition leader Robert Kyagulanyi Ssentamu popularly known as Bobi Wine has condemned the arrest of former legislator Tendai Biti, slamming the Zimbabwean government of shrinking the democratic space. Biti, a convener of the Constitution Defenders Forum (CDF) together with a member Morgan Ncube, was arrested in Mutare over the weekend as they campaigned [...] The post Biti’s arrest a cowardly act says Ugandan opposition leader Bobi Wine appeared first on NewZimbabwe.com .

more.description: A High Court application brought by the National Constitutional Assembly (NCA), its president Lovemore Madhuku, and former Finance Minister Tendai Biti has been postponed to April 1, as tensions mount...
